title: Search and Retrieval
subtitle: Search & Retrieval
description: Aggregates multiple search sources, performs tree-structured recursive deep research, and detects knowledge gaps. Core capability for agents to acquire external knowledge.
sub_problems: Multi-source aggregation: Integrate Web search, academic search, code search, and other sources
Recursive deepening: Auto-generate sub-queries for deeper research when initial search is insufficient
Knowledge gap detection: Identify parts where current information is insufficient to answer
Source credibility: Evaluate trustworthiness and relevance of search results
Deduplication and fusion: Deduplicate and merge results from multiple sources
Engine selection: Auto-select optimal RAG engine based on document type and precision requirements
Component orthogonality: RAG pipeline stages (parsing/chunking/embedding/indexing/retrieval) can be independently replaced without affecting each other
Embedding backend adaptation: Unified interface supporting multiple embedding services like OpenAI/Jina/Cohere/Ollama
best_practices: Tree-structured recursive search is standard for deep research: halve breadth layer-by-layer to control exponential explosion, use visited_urls for deduplication
Dual-engine mutual fallback: Don't rely on single search source, language-aware routing improves Chinese and English search quality
Goal-oriented extraction beats full-text return: Use LLM to extract only information relevant to current goal, saving tokens
Progressive degradation truncation: Better to truncate than fail, three-level degradation 70% → 70% → 25K characters
Independent summarization LLM reduces cost: Use cheaper model for summarization, doesn't occupy main reasoning LLM context
Search strategy must be configurable: Parameterize breadth/depth, different tasks need different search depths
Factory lazy loading avoids dependency pollution: Delayed import via closure, users only install needed RAG backend dependencies
Fluent API pipeline assembly is more flexible than inheritance: Skip unnecessary stages (e.g., LightRAG doesn't need chunker)
Consolidate SERP results using provider-specific Jinja2 templates, AI providers directly use built-in answers
Silent fallback from FAISS to numpy cosine when unavailable, maintain interface consistency

values.DeepTutor: Dual-layer architecture: RAG three engines (LlamaIndex/LightRAG/RAGAnything) + Web six providers
RAG instance caching (_instances dict deduplicated by working_dir), no cross-provider result deduplication
AnswerConsolidator dual strategy: Jinja2 template rendering + LLM summary synthesis

values.DeepResearch: Progressive truncation (70%→70%→25K) + JSON fault tolerance with 3 retries
values.DeepTutor: FAISS→cosine fallback, ImportError capture, KB metadata fallback to env var

values.DeepResearch: Optional independent summarization LLM with 7B model to reduce cost
values.DeepTutor: Three engines tiered by speed/accuracy, embedding adapter supports local Ollama zero-cost option

values.PageIndex: LLM reasoning-based tree search, no vector database, production uses MCTS

values.DeepTutor: FAISS IndexFlatIP + LightRAG knowledge graph + LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex coexist
values.PageIndex: Hierarchical tree index (title/page/summary), auto TOC detection for PDF + MD header parsing

values.DeepTutor: DenseRetriever cosine similarity top-k, LightRAG built-in ranking, Tavily native relevance score
values.PageIndex: LLM reasoning output thinking + node_list, non-similarity-based ranking

values.DeepTutor: RAG instance-level caching (ClassVar dict), no query result caching
values.PageIndex: Tree index built once and persisted as JSON, retrieval passes only tree structure not original text

values.DeepTutor: Component Protocol + decorator registration, add new pipeline/provider with zero core code changes
values.PageIndex: Three multi-document strategies (metadata SQL / semantics DocScore / description LLM)

values.DeepTutor: FileTypeRouter multi-encoding attempts (UTF-8→GBK→Latin-1→replace), binary detection skip
values.PageIndex: Three-level fallback (has TOC with page numbers → has TOC no page numbers → no TOC) + validation fix loop max 3 rounds

values.DeepTutor: RAGAnything supports PDF image/table/formula extraction, MinerU parsing + image migration to canonical path
values.PageIndex: Vision RAG: VLM directly reasons on PDF page images, no OCR needed

values.DeepTutor: ArXiv PaperSearchTool academic search + Serper scholar mode + citation formatting
values.PageIndex: Tree search prompt directly injects domain preference, no embedding fine-tuning needed

solutions.title: DeepResearchSkill Tree-Structured Recursive Search with Halving Breadth and URL Deduplication
solutions.description: DeepResearchSkill in gpt_researcher/skills/deep_research.py implements tree-structured recursive deep research: breadth=4 (4 sub-queries per layer), depth=2 (2 recursion levels), concurrency_limit=2 (asyncio.Semaphore concurrency control). Step 0 generate_research_plan() performs initial search for background → LLM generates 3 clarification questions → constructs combined_query. Each layer generate_search_queries() generates sub-queries → each sub-query creates independent GPTResearcher instance (state isolation) → conduct_research() → process_research_results() extracts learnings + follow-up questions. Breadth halves per layer max(2, breadth//2) to control exponential explosion. Cross-layer shared visited_urls implements URL-level deduplication. Finally trim_context_to_word_limit(25000) truncates and merges results.

solutions.title: Serper Google Search with Zhipu Dual-Engine Routing and Language-Aware Fallback
solutions.description: MiroThinker calls Serper API through two-layer MCP nesting (searching_google_mcp_server.py → serper_mcp_server.py → https://google.serper.dev), essentially a Google search proxy. During migration, skip MCP wrapper and call Serper API directly as new search source in SmartSearchService parallel with Zhipu. Routing strategy: Chinese topics → Zhipu primary + Serper supplements English results, English topics → Serper primary + Zhipu supplements Chinese results. Either engine unavailable → auto fallback. Serper free tier 2500 calls, single blog post uses 3-8 searches. filter_google_search_result() filters search results.

solutions.title: Visit Tool Two-Stage Goal-Oriented Web Extraction with Progressive Degradation Truncation
solutions.description: Visit(BaseTool) in inference/tool_visit.py implements two-stage information extraction pipeline: Stage 1 uses Jina API (https://r.jina.ai/{url}, 3 retries + 50s timeout) to fetch raw Markdown full text → Stage 2 uses independent summarization LLM (SUMMARY_MODEL_NAME env var configurable) + EXTRACTOR_PROMPT for goal-oriented extraction, outputs {rational, evidence, summary} three-field JSON. truncate_to_tokens(text, 95000) uses tiktoken cl100k_base for precise truncation. Summarization failure triggers progressive degradation truncation: 70% → 70% → 25000 characters. JSON fault-tolerant parsing: direct json.loads → extract content between {} → re-invoke LLM (max 3 times).

solutions.title: Three-Engine RAG with Six-Source Search Solution
solutions.description: Switchable three-engine RAG with six-source Web search component architecture, supports selecting optimal retrieval strategy per scenario

solutions.title: Git Repository RAG Retrieval Solution
solutions.description: Understanding a Git repository codebase requires answering various questions

solutions.title: Tree-Structured Vector-Free RAG Retrieval Solution
solutions.description: Traditional RAG systems rely on vector databases for semantic similarity retrieval, with three fundamental limitations
